title: Eva themed Lawson
wikitext:
[]: Eva themed Lawson (SPOILERS?)             Quote Post by Lagann » Sat Apr 24, 2010 3:32 pm Here's some pics of the lawson Eva themed convenient store. How awesome would it be to go here?! http://gigazine.net/index.php?/news/comments/20100423_eva_lawson/ http://gigazine.net/index.php?/news/comments/20100423_eva_lawson_snack/

[]: Re: Eva themed Lawson (SPOILERS?)             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Apr 24, 2010 6:57 pm Lagann wrote: How awesome would it be to go here?!

[]:             Quote Post by cody727kirby » Sat Apr 24, 2010 7:59 pm I knew Eva was popular... but DAMN! Has there been any promotion this huge that happened in the states (with a different franchise, of course)? Maybe Pokemon... Have to admit though... pretty sweet.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Sat Apr 24, 2010 8:50 pm I had no idea Eva was so big. Or I guess the Japanese really take their anime seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Apr 24, 2010 10:30 pm cody727kirby wrote: I knew Eva was popular... but DAMN! Has there been any promotion this huge that happened in the states (with a different franchise, of course)?

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 25, 2010 1:14 am God that's tacky.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Sun Apr 25, 2010 6:42 am What is this I don't even...? Actually, I'd like to know what these are. Babelfish seems to think they're tissue boxes of some sort. Tissue boxes with Kaworu. I'm a very sneezy, hayfever-y girl. And I do like me some Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Apr 25, 2010 8:17 am http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2010-04-25/eva-virtual/real-life-makeover-of-neighborhood-halted Store closed due to Otaku

[]:             Quote Post by IDontWantNoScrub » Sun Apr 25, 2010 9:39 am LMAO!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Apr 25, 2010 10:19 am Hatsumi92 wrote: Store closed due to Otaku

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:11 am ANN wrote: According to LAWSON's announcement on the Hakone project cancellations, its separate nationwide Evangelion campaign will still launch as planned on April 27.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:26 am I realize that the extreme Japanese otaku can be quite insane but to close it simply because they get on people's nerves is a bit extreme don't you think? It's like closing down a month long country music festival due to an influx of rednecks. Edit: Never mind, it appears the otakus flooded in at night, parking their cars in private property and people's driveways. That's pretty annoying. THIS IS WHY WE CAN'T HAVE NICE THINGS

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 25, 2010 11:30 am It really sucks with those otaku having to had park illegal and the like, no wonder the residents of Hakone (lots of old people, Hakone's a resort town) complained. THIS IS WHY WE CAN'T HAVE NICE THINGS! FYI, the store's event stopping has information overlap with this post . This all ties into that 2.22 Hakone event which has been postponed (more in the linked post).

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 25, 2010 2:42 pm Ramiel wrote: What is this I don't even...? Actually, I'd like to know what these are. Babelfish seems to think they're tissue boxes of some sort. Tissue boxes with Kaworu. I'm a very sneezy, hayfever-y girl. And I do like me some Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 25, 2010 3:15 pm SenorSquiid wrote: I'd think there's be a bigger market for Rei themed Kleenex boxes, with commemorative bottle of Hand Lotion.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 25, 2010 3:19 pm NAveryW wrote: A complete set of Haruhi tissue boxes was released a while back. Over $200 for the whole set.

[]:             Quote Post by Peewit » Sun Apr 25, 2010 5:28 pm NAveryW wrote: Over $200 for the whole set.

[]:             Quote Post by Octahedron » Mon Apr 26, 2010 2:03 am It was pretty stupid of them to open this store in a small town that's pretty far from larger cities. Evangelion is very popular so ofcourse you'll end up with a load of people coming to the store, most of them by car due to the distance involved, and you end up with the otaku taking up all availeable parking space and causing a disruption. I hope they open another store in a more reasonable place. Judging from the amount of people coming to this one, it would certainly be profitable.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Mon Apr 26, 2010 2:06 am And some of you question my assertion that advertising and marketing concerns have somehow damaged Eva's artistic integrity. :P I mena sure Simpsons did that too, but this is more like them making a The prisoner themed 7/11. if you aren't familiar with The Prisoner btw, you should be, because if you like Eva you'd like the prisoner, and it's the closest analogue I can think fo in Western TV to eva. Twin Peaks some might say, but Twin Peaks is a totally different animal.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Thu Jul 08, 2010 9:58 am They should make a movie about the experiences of the store clerks in that lawson and call it the Night of the Greasy Otaku. But seriously, for a nation that is almost obsessively polite, how do you get so much people with such complete lack of social skills? They don't even have gaijin invisibility powers.
